story.iWant: to view my calendar in year, month, week, and day perspectives
story.soThat: I can see my synced Google Calendar events at different levels of detail
story.tasks: - Create CalendarEventDisplayModel, NavigationState, ViewMode enum, and EventSelectedMessage - Implement GcalEventRepository (read-only, filters is_deleted) and SystemStateRepository - Implement ColorMappingService stub (Azure #0088CC for all inputs — Story 3.2 completes) - Implement CalendarQueryService (wraps repository, projects GcalEvent to display model, UTC conversion) - Implement NavigationStateService (reads/writes system_state; graceful fallback on failure) - Implement CalendarSelectionService (holds selected ID, publishes EventSelectedMessage) - Build MainViewModel with SwitchViewMode, NavigatePrevious, NavigateNext, NavigateToday, JumpToDate commands and breadcrumb computation - Build MainPage.xaml shell (toolbar: view toggle, prev/next/today, breadcrumb, jump-to-date, settings) - Build YearViewControl, MonthViewControl, WeekViewControl, DayViewControl - Register all new services and views in App.xaml.cs; switch window entry point from SettingsPage to MainPage

acceptanceCriteria.ac: Given the app launches with a populated gcal_event table, the year view is displayed showing 12 months with day cells for the current year.
Given the user clicks the Month toggle, the view switches to a month grid displaying all events for the current month with event titles visible on each day cell.
Given the user clicks the Week toggle, the view switches to a 7-column hourly timeline for the current week with events positioned at their correct time slots.
Given the user clicks the Day toggle, the view switches to a single-day hourly timeline with full-width event blocks.
Given any view is active, switching between views completes with a smooth animation in under 300 ms.
Given the app restarts, it opens to the last viewed view mode and date restored from system_state keys current_view_mode and current_view_date.
Given a month view with 200+ events across the month, the view renders in under 1 second.
Given no events are synced yet, all calendar views show the empty state message "No events synced yet. Go to Settings → Connect Google Calendar to sync." without crashing.

doc.title: Epic 3 Technical Specification
doc.section: Detailed Design — Services, Data Models, XAML View Composition, Workflows, NFRs
doc.snippet: Defines all service interfaces, CalendarEventDisplayModel record, NavigationState, ViewMode, the 7 interaction flows, performance targets, and XAML layout hierarchy. Primary technical reference for this story.

doc.title: Epic Breakdown
doc.section: Epic 3 — Story 3.1 requirements and technical notes
doc.snippet: Story 3.1 user story statement, acceptance criteria, and keyboard shortcut requirements (Y/M/W/D keys). Cross-reference with tech-spec for authoritative design.

doc.title: UX Design Specification
doc.section: Section 2 Core UX Principles, Section 3 Visual Foundation, Section 6.1 Tier 1 Components
doc.snippet: Visual state language: published events at 100% opacity, selected events get 2px red outline (Story 3.3). Year/Month/Week/Day view layout descriptions and top strip controls spec.

doc.title: Architecture Document
doc.section: Project Structure, Technology Stack, Epic to Architecture Mapping
doc.snippet: WARNING: Architecture doc describes a hypothetical src/ folder hierarchy. Actual project is flat — Views/, ViewModels/, Services/, Models/, Data/ all at project root. Follow actual structure, not the doc.

doc.title: Story 2.3A (last completed story)
doc.section: Dev Notes — Architecture Patterns and Constraints
doc.snippet: Critical learnings: use IDbContextFactory not scoped CalendarDbContext in singletons; GcalEventId is a string PK not int; all timestamps are UTC; soft-delete means filter IsDeleted == false always.

file.reason: DI container (ConfigureServices) must be extended with all new services, repositories, and views. Entry point currently sets window.Content = settingsPage — must change to mainPage with InitializeAsync call.

Primary data source for calendar views. Key fields: GcalEventId (string PK), Summary, StartDatetime, EndDatetime, IsAllDay, ColorId, IsDeleted (filter false), IsRecurringInstance, LastSyncedAt, Description.

Persistence target for NavigationStateService. Uses StateName/StateValue key-value pairs. Keys: current_view_mode (string), current_view_date (ISO date). Check SystemStateConfiguration.cs for actual column names.
Provides LastRefreshedAt for the last-synced timestamp shown in the event details panel (Story 3.4). Referenced in CalendarEventDisplayModel.LastSyncedAt via GcalEvent.LastSyncedAt.
EF Core context. Use IDbContextFactory<CalendarDbContext> (already registered) in all singleton repository implementations — never inject the scoped CalendarDbContext into singletons.
Reveals actual DB column names for system_state table. Verify StateName maps to state_name before writing SystemStateRepository queries.
Established ViewModel pattern: constructor injection, ObservableObject base, ILogger usage. Follow same structure for MainViewModel.
Template for EventSelectedMessage. Same Messages/ folder, same namespace GoogleCalendarManagement.Messages, same WeakReferenceMessenger.Default usage pattern.
Shows established integration test pattern: IDbContextFactory fixture, seeded SQLite DB, FluentAssertions. Extend this pattern for CalendarQueryServiceTests and NavigationStateRoundTripTests.

package.name: Microsoft.WindowsAppSDK
package.use: WinUI 3 controls: Frame, ScrollViewer, ItemsRepeater, UniformGrid, CalendarDatePicker, RadioButtons, ThemeAnimation

package.name: Microsoft.EntityFrameworkCore.Sqlite
package.use: IDbContextFactory for all repository DB access

dotnet.newPackagesNeeded: None — all required packages are already present from Epics 1 and 2.

constraints.constraint: Project structure is FLAT at root — Views/, ViewModels/, Services/, Models/, Data/, Messages/ are all direct children of the project root. Do NOT create a src/ folder or Core/ library. The architecture doc's src/ layout is aspirational, not actual.

App.xaml.cs line 131 currently sets window.Content = settingsPage. This story MUST change this to window.Content = mainPage with a call to mainPage.ViewModel.InitializeAsync() after activation. SettingsPage remains accessible via a settings button in the MainPage toolbar.
ColorMappingService is a STUB in Story 3.1 — return "#0088CC" for all inputs. Do not implement the 9-colour dictionary here. Story 3.2 owns the full colour mapping implementation.

ISyncStatusService does NOT exist yet — Story 2.4 owns it and is ready-for-dev but not implemented. Story 3.1 must NOT define or call ISyncStatusService. Year view day cells show a static grey dot placeholder with a TODO comment pointing to Story 2.4.

GcalEvent.GcalEventId is a string (natural Google event ID), NOT an int. CalendarEventDisplayModel and CalendarSelectionService must use string IDs throughout. Do not assume or add an integer PK to GcalEvent.

All repository implementations are registered as singletons. NEVER inject the scoped CalendarDbContext into a singleton service. Use IDbContextFactory<CalendarDbContext> and create a fresh context per operation: await using var db = await _dbFactory.CreateDbContextAsync(ct);
ALL GcalEvent queries must filter where IsDeleted == false. Soft-deleted events must never appear in any calendar view.

UTC to local time conversion belongs in CalendarQueryService during GcalEvent → CalendarEventDisplayModel projection, NOT in views or ViewModels. Call .ToLocalTime() on StartDatetime and EndDatetime during projection.
Week starts on Monday (ISO 8601). DayOfWeek.Sunday = 0 in .NET — adjust accordingly. Formula: daysFromMonday = (dayOfWeek == 0) ? 6 : dayOfWeek - 1; monday = date.AddDays(-daysFromMonday).

Story 3.1 is Tier 1 (read-only). Do not implement event selection outline (Story 3.3), event details panel (Story 3.4), event editing (Story 3.5), event creation (Story 3.6), or colour picker (Story 3.7).

tests.standards: xUnit + FluentAssertions for all tests. Integration tests use IDbContextFactory with SQLite (same pattern as GoogleCalendarSyncTests.cs). No mocking of EF Core — seed real in-memory SQLite.

ideas.test: Call GetHexColor(null) and assert result is "#0088CC" without throwing.
Call GetHexColor("purple"), GetHexColor("9"), GetHexColor("unknown") and assert all return "#0088CC" in Story 3.1.
Call LoadAsync on an empty system_state table and assert result is NavigationState(ViewMode.Year, DateOnly.FromDateTime(DateTime.Today)).
Save a NavigationState(ViewMode.Week, new DateOnly(2026, 3, 15)), then load, and assert identical values returned.
Set CurrentDate = 2026-01-15, switch to Month view, assert DateFrom = 2026-01-01 and DateTo = 2026-01-31.
Set CurrentDate = 2026-03-26 (Thursday), switch to Week view, assert DateFrom = 2026-03-23 (Monday) and DateTo = 2026-03-29 (Sunday).
Set CurrentDate = 2026-01-15, view = Month, call NavigatePrevious, assert CurrentDate.Month = 12 and CurrentDate.Year = 2025.
Set CurrentDate = 2026-06-15, view = Year, assert BreadcrumbLabel = "2026".
Set CurrentDate = 2026-01-15 (Thursday, week Mon 13 – Sun 19), view = Week, assert BreadcrumbLabel contains "Jan 13" and "19".
Seed 50 GcalEvent rows across January 2026; call GetEventsForRangeAsync(Jan 1, Jan 31); assert count matches seeded events in range.
Seed 3 GcalEvent rows; mark 1 as IsDeleted = true; call GetEventsForRangeAsync; assert only 2 returned.
Seed a GcalEvent with Summary = null; call GetEventsForRangeAsync; assert returned CalendarEventDisplayModel.Title == "".
